$comment: Datasheet Schema v1.0 — Apache-2.0 — https://github.com/nkorai/datasheet-schema. This file is self-contained: copy it anywhere and it validates with no external $refs.
title: Datasheet Schema
description: A machine-readable, design-grade schema for electronic-component datasheet specifications. Unlike parametric databases, every value carries the TEST CONDITIONS it was measured under and PROVENANCE back to the datasheet page — the two things that make a spec safe to design from, and the two things no existing standard captures.

schemaVersion.description: The Datasheet Schema version this document conforms to.

pinout.description: Physical pins with normalized functions, so a tool can wire the part without string-matching vendor pin names.

parameters.description: EVERY specified value, unified. Absolute-maximum, recommended-operating, electrical, thermal, and ESD data all live here as parameters, distinguished by each measurement's `limitClass` rather than by separate tables. This is what lets one envelope describe any component family.

notes.description: Anything ambiguous, conflicting, or spread across variant tables that a human reviewer should know.

component.title: Component identity
component.description: Who the part is. Borrows the Octopart/GS1 identity envelope.

family.description: Component family. Determines which parameter dictionary applies.

orderingVariants.description: The orderable variants covered by this datasheet.

outputVoltageV.description: Fixed output option, when this variant pins a voltage.

parameter.title: Parameter
parameter.description: One canonically-keyed characteristic. A family dictionary (e.g. dictionary/ldo-1.0.json) defines the legal keys, their units, and vendor aliases — but the schema keeps the parameter SHAPE universal so every family reuses it.

key.description: Canonical parameter key from the family dictionary, e.g. "dropout_voltage", "psrr", "reference_voltage", "thermal_resistance_junction_ambient".

aliases.description: Vendor synonyms for this parameter (e.g. psrr ↔ "ripple rejection"). Normalizing naming in the schema, not in downstream code, is what keeps this interoperable across manufacturers.

measurements.description: One entry per distinct condition set. A parameter specified across several load currents or frequencies (dropout, PSRR, quiescent current) has one measurement per point.

measurement.title: Measurement
measurement.description: The design-grade atom: a value + unit + the conditions it holds under + which table it came from + provenance.

limitClass.description: Which kind of limit this is — the distinction no other standard encodes. `absolute_max` is a stress limit (damage beyond it). `recommended` is the specified operating range. `characterized` is a measured/guaranteed electrical characteristic. Confusing absolute_max with recommended burns boards, so it is first-class here.

conditions.description: The test conditions this value holds under — the product's moat. An open list of typed axes so it generalizes across families (an LDO uses I_OUT/V_IN; a MOSFET uses V_GS/V_DS; a buck adds F_SW). At least the axes that govern the value should be present.

stimulus.description: For dynamic parameters (load/line transient, start-up), the applied step. Modeled once here because the from/to/slew triple recurs for load steps, line steps, and MOSFET switching.

conditionsVerbatim.description: The test conditions exactly as printed in the datasheet. Kept alongside the parsed axes so nothing is ever lost in normalization.

sourcePage.description: 1-indexed datasheet page this value was read from.

sourceTable.description: The table it came from, e.g. "Electrical Characteristics".

allOf.$comment: A characterized/recommended value should say where it came from; abs-max stress rows sometimes have no test conditions.

value.title: Value
value.description: IEC 61360 levelType roles. `nom` is a nominal/target (e.g. a fixed 3.3 V option); `typ` is a typical measured value. At least one role is present.

conditionAxis.title: Condition axis
conditionAxis.description: One test-condition dimension. `param` uses a controlled vocabulary where possible (see spec) but any string is allowed so family-specific axes extend it. Give a scalar `value`, or a `min`/`max` range.

param.description: The condition dimension. Recommended vocabulary: T_J, T_A, V_IN, V_OUT, I_OUT, I_LOAD, F, C_OUT, C_IN, ESR, HEADROOM, RIPPLE, BW_LOW, BW_HIGH, V_EN, C_OUT_TYPE. Family-specific axes (V_GS, V_DS, F_SW, ...) are permitted.

note.description: Free text, e.g. a capacitor type "X7R ceramic".

stimulus.title: Stimulus
stimulus.description: An applied step for a dynamic measurement: e.g. I_OUT from 0 to 300 mA at 0.2 A/us.

param.description: What is stepped, e.g. I_OUT, V_IN.

slewUnit.description: Unit of the slew rate, e.g. "A/us", "V/us".

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: The audit trail — where every value in this document ultimately came from. No existing standard carries this. It is what makes a spec verifiable rather than merely asserted.

datasheetSha256.description: SHA-256 of the exact datasheet PDF bytes.

sourceUrl.description: Where the datasheet was fetched from.

datasheetRevision.description: Revision string as printed, e.g. "SBVS176K — REVISED MARCH 2023".

extractionMethod.description: How the data was produced, e.g. "manual", "llm:claude-sonnet-4-5".

confidence.description: 0-1 extraction confidence. Advisory only — does not by itself imply the data is verified.

verified.description: True only when the data passed a validation suite.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Base-SI unit symbol. Values are normalized to base units (volts, amperes, hertz, ...) so cross-part numeric comparison is always sound. Derived units are included where a datasheet parameter needs them (tempco ppm/degC, slew V/us).
